what_it_does: Defines an HTML page that renders a landscape-oriented decision card by loading shared styles, fetching layout definitions, creating an inline decision record, and passing it to DNA.renderArchetype.
observations: The page title and caption identify it as a system-drawn landscape decision surface.
It imports Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts and shared CSS from /surface/styles.
Local CSS frames the page with a warm background, centered mount area, caption text, rounded screen container, and shadow.
The inline record describes a pending binary decision at decision://global/merge-sa-authorize with two options: Absorb and Fork.
The script fetches /dna/layouts.json and renders layouts.archetypes["decision-card"] with orient:"landscape".
A resolve callback supplies explanation_source and state values to the renderer.
standouts: The file demonstrates orientation as a renderer option while keeping the decision record unchanged.
The content explicitly describes native landscape reflow rather than scaling a phone layout.
The decision data, explanatory text, and renderer invocation are all contained in the page.
conventions: Uses a minimal HTML document with viewport metadata and linked shared design-system CSS.
Uses design tokens through CSS variables for spacing, radius, type, and color.
Uses an async IIFE to fetch data and render after page load.
Uses absolute paths for shared runtime, style, and layout assets.
Keeps page-specific framing CSS inline in the document head.
concerns: The page depends on global DNA being available from /surface/runtime/archetype.js.
There is no error handling for failed layout fetches or missing archetype data.
The explanatory copy is hardcoded separately from the record data, creating two local content sources.
The caption contains a possessive apostrophe in "archetype's" while most other prose is typographic and editorial.
notes: A self-contained landscape demo page for rendering one decision-card record through the shared archetype runtime.
questions: What exact fields does the decision-card archetype expect from the record and resolver?
Is the caption intended as part of the artifact or only as surrounding documentation?
Should explanation_source always resolve from a separate string rather than from the record itself?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it presents the same decision card as a system-drawn landscape rendering with orientation-driven reflow.
intention_signals: It seems intended to show that a structured decision record can be rendered by a shared archetype runtime in landscape orientation without changing the underlying content.
novelty: The page treats orientation as a first-class render parameter for a decision substrate, with the prose emphasizing native reflow of unchanged content.
contribution: A concrete landscape decision-card substrate: page framing, inline decision identity/options/explanation data, resolver behavior, and a render call that preserves content while changing orientation.
summary_for_embedding: This is an HTML demo page that renders a pending binary decision card in landscape orientation using a shared DNA archetype renderer and inline decision data.
intention_for_embedding: It was for demonstrating native landscape reflow of a system-drawn decision card while keeping the decision record unchanged.
